raw_consumer_statement:
I booked an XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ' ) in XXXX , arriving in the evening on XX/XX/year>. XXXX XXXX turned out to be not suitable for renting for a number of reasons ( mainly odour, cleanliness, and security ), and XXXX promptly refunded my money and suggested I rebook. This was around XXXX in downtown XXXX. 

Once out of XXXX XXXX, I attempted to book a different XXXX in XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ' ), and was provided with an automated reply containing a link to a third-party form by the host I was attempting to book with. The form link was suspect and not associated with XXXX, and requested pictures of ID and other details I was uncomfortable with. Regardless, I attempted to fill out the form for XXXX XXXX, however I received an error everytime I tried to proceed past the first step. I could not upload any pictures as required by the form, although I tried on several different networks using different browsers. I attempted to contact the host of XXXX XXXX immediately to resolve this ( now about XXXX ) but they were non-responsive. This is all documented. I contacted XXXX, who in turn attempted to reach the host but could not. We tried to do this as midnight approached, however XXXX recommended I attempt booking elsewhere, which I successfully did ( XXXX XXXX ' ). 

I was never provided check in instructions, a full address, or any means of checking into XXXX XXXX, however I was still charged a full amount. XXXX and AMEX have both simply sent along copies of XXXX 's no cancellation policies associated with XXXX XXXX, however there were never check in instructions provided to me. XXXX has since offered to provide a 20 % refund to me, however I am seeking a full refund.
